body:
### Why this matters

Azure App Service Node.js runtimes are periodically deprecated when the upstream language version ages out or has significant vulnerabilities. Apps can keep running for a while after a runtime is hidden from the portal, but future platform removal can create urgent deployment or availability risk.

### What this control checks

This control finds App Service apps using a deprecated Node.js stack according to the runtime metadata collected from Azure.

### Operational note

Runtime upgrades should be released like application changes: update dependencies, test startup and build behavior, use deployment slots for production apps, and validate telemetry after the swap.
name: Ensure App Service Node.js runtime is currently supported
description: App Service apps using deprecated Node.js stacks
timedControlSummary: Azure App Service apps using unsupported Node.js stacks should be moved to a currently supported runtime.
timedControlDisruptionRisk: Late Node.js runtime migrations can break builds, dependencies, native modules, and deployment workflows when the platform removes old runtimes.
remediationDescription:
## Azure remediation

> **Service-wide runtime upgrade (recommended):** upgrade all App Service Node.js apps in an application portfolio together where possible. This makes dependency updates, package-lock refreshes, build image changes, and CI/CD validation reusable.

### Azure Portal

1. Open the affected App Service app.
2. Go to **Settings > Configuration > General settings**.
3. Change the Node.js runtime stack to a supported version, such as Node.js 24 LTS.
4. Save, redeploy if required, and validate startup, health checks, and logs.

### Azure CLI

Check the current runtime:

```bash
az webapp config show \
  --resource-group <resource-group> \
  --name <app-name> \
  --query '{linuxFxVersion:linuxFxVersion,nodeVersion:nodeVersion}'
```

List supported runtimes:

```bash
az webapp list-runtimes --os linux | grep NODE
az webapp list-runtimes --os windows | grep NODE
```

For Linux App Service apps, set the supported runtime stack:

```bash
az webapp config set \
  --resource-group <resource-group> \
  --name <app-name> \
  --linux-fx-version "NODE|24-lts"
```

For Windows App Service apps, set the app setting:

```bash
az webapp config appsettings set \
  --resource-group <resource-group> \
  --name <app-name> \
  --settings WEBSITE_NODE_DEFAULT_VERSION="~24"
```

### References

- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/app-service/configure-language-nodejs

timedControlRules.summary: Azure App Service apps using unsupported Node.js stacks should be moved to a currently supported runtime.
timedControlRules.disruptionRisk: Late Node.js runtime migrations can break builds, dependencies, native modules, and deployment workflows when the platform removes old runtimes.
timedControlRules.recommendedTarget: Set Node.js to a supported App Service runtime, for example NODE|24-lts on Linux or WEBSITE_NODE_DEFAULT_VERSION=~24 on Windows.
